messages.content: Build a pure Decider for a ResearchStudy aggregate in TypeScript. Export initialState, decide, and evolve with discriminated unions for Command, Event, and State. Throw DomainError when closing a study that still has open enrolments.

Write an event-sourced Decider for a SpecimenKit aggregate. Constraints: strict types, no any, exhaustive switch with never default, typed DomainError, reject dispatching a kit that has not been sealed.

Explain briefly how a Decider captures the lifecycle of a ConsentPackage, then show a typed implementation with draft/signed/revoked states.

A Decider packages three pure functions: initialState is a literal, decide(command, state) returns events or throws a typed error without doing I/O, and evolve(state, event) folds events back into state using an exhaustive switch. The outer adapter loads the stream and appends returned events.
What's the idiomatic way to model a PublicationDraft Decider with submit, review, accept, and reject commands? Show a minimal typed example.
Build a Decider for an IRBApproval aggregate. Constraints: no any, decide rejects amending an already expired approval, exhaustive never default, typed DomainError.
Build a pure Decider for a DataRelease aggregate. Commands: prepare, approve, publish, embargo. Throw InvariantError when publishing a release still under embargo.
Implement a projection for ResearchStudy events that tracks enrolment counts and closed-study totals per principal investigator.
Write a projection that folds LabSample events into a per-status count view. Ignore events that do not change status.
Show a minimal projection for PublicationDraft events that tracks the latest status per draft id in a map.
Build a read-model projection for IRBApproval events that surfaces approvals expiring within the next 30 days. Ignore submission events. Return the view unchanged for events outside that concern.

Implement Snapshot plus versioned upgrade for SpecimenKit state. Stored v1 had `count:string`, v2 renamed to `items:number`, v3 added `storageTempC:number`. Write v1ToV2 and v2ToV3 upgraders and a loader.
Write a Snapshot loader for a ConsentPackage with two upgraders: v1 had `subjectName:string`, v2 split into `subjectId:string` plus `subjectName`, v3 added `clauseCount:number`.

Explain briefly how versioned Snapshot upgraders work, then show a typed loader for a BiobankEntry with three schema versions.

A Snapshot pairs state with a numeric version tag. When the stored version is older than the current schema, a chain of small upgraders (v1ToV2, v2ToV3) advances the state one step at a time. The loader inspects the version, runs each upgrader if needed, and returns a snapshot tagged with CURRENT_VERSION. Unknown versions fail loudly.
Show how to guard an appendToStream on a ResearchStudy with expectedRevision. Surface a typed ConcurrencyConflict when the server reports a mismatch.
Build an appendGuarded helper for a PreprintVersion stream using optimistic concurrency with expectedRevision. Keep the EventStore interface local.
Write an Effect-wrapped appendToStream helper for DataRelease events that converts store conflicts into a tagged ConcurrencyConflict failure.
Build a CommandHandler for a LabSample. Orchestrate load, decide, append. Keep business logic inside decide/evolve only.
Write an Effect-wrapped CommandHandler for a ConsentPackage. Use Effect.gen to orchestrate readStream, reduce, decide, and appendToStream.

Explain briefly the role of a CommandHandler, then show a typed handler for a SpecimenKit that loads the stream, decides, and appends under optimistic concurrency.

The CommandHandler is the orchestration seam between pure domain code and the event store. It loads the stream, folds events with evolve to rebuild state, calls decide to obtain new events, and appends them under the revision it just read. All invariants live in decide; the handler is plumbing.
Build a CommandHandler for a CohortRegister that surfaces ConcurrencyConflict as a typed error. Load with readStream, decide, then appendToStream.
Write a subscription runner that resumes a LabSample projection from the last persisted checkpoint, applies each event, then writes the checkpoint.
Implement a crash-safe subscription from checkpoint that projects IRBApproval events. The checkpoint write must happen after the view has been saved.
Show a resumable Effect.gen subscription for a DataRelease projection. The checkpoint is written only after the projection is persisted.
Implement a generic aggregateStream helper and use it to rebuild a ResearchStudy state from an AsyncIterable of events with a typed when reducer.
Show how to use aggregateStream to reconstruct an AnalysisPipeline state from a stream of events.
Build an aggregateStream-based loader for a BiobankEntry with a pure when reducer.
Use aggregateStream with Effect.gen to rebuild a PublicationDraft aggregate. Keep when pure and wrap only the I/O.
Write an upcaster that migrates an old ResearchStudy event shape to the current one. V1 used `investigator`; v2 renamed it to `pi`. Throw UnknownEventError on unrecognized types.
How do I migrate an old LabSample event shape at read time? V1 had `sampleId`; v2 renamed to `id` and added `barcode`. Show a typed upcast function.
Write a read-model rebuild loop that re-projects SpecimenKit events from revision 0 in batches. Provide a from option for resumability and a progress callback.
Show a chunked rebuild for a CohortRegister read model. The loop must be resumable via a from cursor and persist after each batch.
Implement a minimal CQRS boundary for an IRBApproval. handleCommand goes through a Decider + EventStore; queryView reads from a separate read store. The read store is eventually consistent.

Build a CQRS split for a DataRelease: handleCommand writes via a Decider + EventStore; queryDataRelease reads from a view store. Event DU shared, state types distinct.

Events are the contract between write and read sides. The write state enforces invariants; the read view is flat and query-optimized. The read store converges asynchronously via a projection behind a subscription.

Build a stateless process-manager react function. Given a ResearchStudy event stream, emit the next command so that each Registered triggers a ConsentPackage draft and each Closed triggers a DataRelease prepare. Return null for events the saga ignores.

Explain briefly how a process-manager saga reacts to events by emitting the next command, then show a 3-step saga: IRBApprovalGranted leads to SpecimenKitAssembled which leads to LabSampleReceived.

A stateless process manager is a pure function from event to next command (or null). It wires aggregates together by subscribing to one stream and emitting commands to another. The current step is encoded in the last event the saga observed, so no internal state is required.
Write a pure translate function from a DataRelease domain event to an IntegrationEvent posted to an outbox. Return null for events that must not leave the service.
Build an outbox helper that atomically appends PublicationDraft events to a stream and inserts integration rows. A polling worker publishes them later; call that out in a comment.

Add an expire-after-grace capability to this valid ConsentPackage decide. It currently supports draft, sign, and revoke but we need revoke to be allowed only within 30 days and to emit ConsentPackageExpired instead when past the grace period.

```typescript
type C = { type: 'draft'; id: string; subject: string } | { type: 'sign'; at: string } | { type: 'revoke'; at: string };
```
